title: Implement tool_follow_ups! macro for follow-up action registration
purpose: Implement the tool_follow_ups! proc macro that generates follow-up action registration code. Using the FollowUpAction and ExpiryCondition types from norn::tool::follow_up (defined by NTF-001), this macro lets tool authors declare follow-up actions declaratively. The macro generates Vec<FollowUpAction> construction with condition evaluation, expiry tracking, and description interpolation.
task: Using the FollowUpAction and ExpiryCondition types from norn::tool::follow_up (defined by NTF-001), replace the NTM-001 no-op tool_follow_ups placeholder with the real proc macro implementation in crates/norn-macros/src/follow_up/. The macro parses declarative action definitions with name, description, when (condition closure), expires (shorthand syntax), and overrides (JSON literal). It generates code that evaluates conditions against ToolOutput and constructs Vec<FollowUpAction>.

requirements.title: Parse follow-up action declarations
requirements.spec: The follow_up/parse.rs module SHALL parse the tool_follow_ups! macro input into a structured representation. Each action declaration has: tool (type path), and an actions array. Each action has: name (string literal), description (string literal or format string), when (closure taking &ToolOutput -> bool), expires (shorthand expression), and overrides (JSON literal via braces).
acceptance: crates/norn-macros/src/follow_up/parse.rs exists
Parses tool: EditTool as a type path
Parses name: "undo" as a string literal
Parses description with format-string-style interpolation like "Revert {path}"
Parses when: |output| output.content["committed"] == true as a closure expression
Parses expires: FileModified(expr) as a shorthand
Parses overrides: { "key": "value" } as a JSON-like token tree

requirements.title: Generate follow-up action construction code
requirements.spec: The follow_up/codegen.rs module SHALL generate a function that accepts &ToolOutput and returns Vec<FollowUpAction>. For each action, the generated code SHALL: evaluate the when condition, and if true, construct a FollowUpAction with the name, interpolated description, expiry condition, and overrides. The generated code SHALL reference ::norn::tool::follow_up::FollowUpAction and ::norn::tool::follow_up::ExpiryCondition by absolute path.
acceptance: crates/norn-macros/src/follow_up/codegen.rs exists
Generated code evaluates when closures against the ToolOutput reference
Actions whose when condition returns false are excluded from the result Vec
Generated code constructs FollowUpAction with all 4 fields populated
Generated code references types by absolute path (::norn::tool::follow_up::...)

requirements.title: Implement expiry shorthand expansion
requirements.spec: The macro SHALL provide shorthand syntax for expiry conditions: FileModified(path_expr) expands to ExpiryCondition::FileModified { path: path_expr.to_string(), modified_at: ... }. AnyFileModified expands to ExpiryCondition::AnyFileModified { ... }. TurnScoped expands to ExpiryCondition::TurnScoped { ... }. Never expands to ExpiryCondition::Never. The modified_at timestamp SHALL be captured at registration time via chrono::Utc::now().
acceptance: FileModified(output.content["path"].as_str()) generates ExpiryCondition::FileModified with the path extracted from output
AnyFileModified generates ExpiryCondition::AnyFileModified
TurnScoped generates ExpiryCondition::TurnScoped
Never generates ExpiryCondition::Never
The shorthand names match the ExpiryCondition variant names exactly

requirements.title: Implement description interpolation
requirements.spec: WHEN a follow-up action description contains {field_name} placeholders, the generated code SHALL interpolate them using values extracted from the ToolOutput content. Interpolation SHALL use format!() with values obtained via output.content["field_name"]. Missing fields SHALL produce the literal string "<missing>" rather than panicking.
acceptance: Description "Revert {path} to pre-edit content" generates format!("Revert {} to pre-edit content", output.content["path"].as_str().unwrap_or("<missing>"))
Description with no placeholders passes through as a plain string literal
Multiple placeholders in one description all get interpolated
A placeholder referencing a missing output field produces "<missing>" in the description

requirements.title: Implement overrides as JSON literal
requirements.spec: The overrides field in an action declaration SHALL accept a JSON-like literal in braces. The macro SHALL expand it to a serde_json::json!({...}) call in the generated code. An empty overrides ({}) SHALL produce serde_json::json!({}).
acceptance: overrides: { "mode": "structural" } generates serde_json::json!({"mode": "structural"})
overrides: {} generates serde_json::json!({})
Nested overrides { "flags": ["allow_broken_ast"] } generate the correct nested JSON

requirements.title: Wire follow_up/mod.rs and replace NTM-001 placeholder
requirements.spec: follow_up/mod.rs SHALL export the proc macro implementation function. The #[proc_macro] tool_follow_ups entry point in lib.rs SHALL delegate to follow_up::expand instead of returning an empty TokenStream. The NTM-001 placeholder SHALL be fully replaced.
acceptance: crates/norn-macros/src/follow_up/mod.rs exports pub fn expand(input: TokenStream) -> TokenStream
lib.rs tool_follow_ups calls follow_up::expand
The placeholder empty TokenStream from NTM-001 is removed
cargo check -p norn-macros succeeds

requirements.title: Integration tests for follow-up macro
requirements.spec: Integration tests SHALL verify the tool_follow_ups! macro generates correct follow-up action registration code. Tests SHALL cover: single action, multiple actions with conditions, description interpolation, all four expiry shorthands, and overrides.
acceptance: Test: single action with when: |_| true produces Vec with 1 FollowUpAction
Test: two actions where one condition is false produces Vec with 1 FollowUpAction
Test: description with {field} placeholder interpolates from mock ToolOutput
Test: FileModified expiry shorthand produces ExpiryCondition::FileModified
Test: Never expiry shorthand produces ExpiryCondition::Never
Test: overrides with {"mode": "structural"} produces correct serde_json::Value
All tests pass with cargo test -p norn-macros

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement the follow-up tool itself (follow_up tool that executes actions by tool_call_id) — that is a separate norn-tools/follow-up brief
SHALL NOT implement expiry checking logic (comparing timestamps, detecting file modifications) — that is follow-up tool execution logic
SHALL NOT implement action log storage or indexing — that is norn-tools/action-log
SHALL NOT modify any existing tool implementations to add follow-up actions — individual tool follow-up wiring is future work after the follow-up tool itself is built
SHALL NOT add the register_follow_ups method to the Tool trait — that lifecycle extension is part of the follow-up design cluster, not this crate setup
SHALL NOT define FollowUpAction or ExpiryCondition types — those are defined by NTF-001 in norn::tool::follow_up
verification: cargo check -p norn-macros succeeds with zero errors
cargo check -p norn succeeds with zero errors
cargo clippy -p norn-macros -- -D warnings produces zero errors
cargo clippy -p norn -- -D warnings produces zero errors
cargo test -p norn-macros passes all follow-up tests
The tool_follow_ups! macro compiles and generates correct Vec<FollowUpAction> construction code referencing types from norn::tool::follow_up
